Jeg finansierte menigheten selv, jobbet og betalte for den, bygde den for lokalsamfunnet. Jeg elsker Gud, og det er til Ham min kjærlighet går.

text:
So, I remember that night, she went over. And we was all sitting around there, and I said, "All right, now tonight, friends, I hate to ask you this," I seen her look up at me. And I was trying to keep my head turned from her. I said, "You know, it's just one of these times, I never did this before, it's you know, I can't hardly make ends meet, you know how it gets." I said, "You all got a nickel or a dime," I said, "you'd like in my hat over here?" I said, "Brother Wisehart, would you come and take my hat and…
So, Brother Wisehart, didn't hardly know what to do. He come over there at my hat, I looked down and his little old mother sitting down, reached down there with this little apron, you know, little checker apron, pulled out this little pocketbook, had a snap on the top of it, and begin to reach down and get… Oh, me! I couldn't take that. I thought, that poor old woman sitting there with that money reaching them nickels in there. Huh-uh! I thought, "Oh!"
I said, "I was just teasing you. I wanted to see what you'd do." So then Brother Wisehart, already had my hat, you know."
He said, "Brother Branham, what am I to do?"
I said, "Just put my hat back. I was just going on, Brother Wisehart." And I seen my wife, shook her head and kind of laughed.
Brother Ryan give me an old bicycle. He had been down there, and I … and left give me an old bicycle, and I painted it up and sold it for ten dollars, didn't have to take an offering after all. That was the same bicycle on when that infidel met me on the corner and said, what he did, [unclear words] to me. I'm packing his pocketbook here today. And how God worked there.
Oh, it's wonderful to see Him in His power. Then, I… One day, coming home, I was … I had been studying. Many of the ministers had been telling me, not to listen to such stuff as that. I have to hurry. Just got about twenty-seven minutes now. And I will hurry as quick as I can, because I want to get in an African experience. And this brother sitting here from Africa could get hold of now.
And now, I remembered this, that I said, "I wasn't going to have no more to do with it." And one day, I was going up the road to patrol, up at the Henryville State Forestry. And I got on the bus, and now, listen everyone of you, closely. The strange thing it was, that every time I meet a demon possessed person, like a fortune teller, or something, they'd speak to me, and seem to notice. And then that would scare me to death.
text_no:
Jeg husker den kvelden da hun kom over. Vi satt alle rundt der, og jeg sa: "Godt, kjære venner, i kveld, jeg hater å spørre dere om dette," og jeg så henne kikke opp på meg. Jeg prøvde å vende hodet bort fra henne. Jeg sa: "Dette er en av de gangene jeg aldri har gjort dette før, det er slik, jeg klarer knapt å få endene til å møtes, dere vet hvordan det er." Jeg sa, "Har dere en femøring eller en tikrone," jeg sa, "som dere vil legge i hatten min her?" Jeg sa, "Bror Wisehart, kan du komme og ta hatten min...?"
Bror Wisehart visste knapt hva han skulle gjøre. Han kom bort til hatten min, og jeg kikket ned. Hans gamle mor satt der, bøyde seg ned med det lille forkleet med ruter, trakk ut en liten lommebok med en knapp på toppen, og begynte å hente opp noen mynter. Åh, nei! Jeg kunne ikke ta imot det. Jeg tenkte, den stakkars gamle kvinnen, sittende der med sine penger, nådde etter femøringene. Nei! Jeg tenkte, "Åh!"
Jeg sa, "Jeg bare tullet med dere. Jeg ville se hva dere ville gjøre." Så bror Wisehart hadde allerede hatten min, dere vet.
Han sa, "Bror Branham, hva skal jeg gjøre?"
Jeg sa, "Bare sett hatten tilbake. Jeg bare tullet, bror Wisehart." Og jeg så min kone riste på hodet og smile litt.
Bror Ryan ga meg en gammel sykkel. Han hadde vært nede der, og jeg... han ga meg en gammel sykkel, og jeg malte den opp og solgte den for ti dollar. Jeg slapp å ta en kollekt likevel. Det var den samme sykkelen da den gudsfornekteren møtte meg på hjørnet og sa hva han gjorde. Jeg har hans lommebok her i dag. Og hvordan Gud virket der.
Oh, det er herlig å se Ham i Sin kraft. Så, en dag, på vei hjem, hadde jeg studert. Mange av predikantene hadde fortalt meg at jeg ikke skulle høre på slike ting. Jeg må skynde meg. Har bare omtrent tjuesyv minutter nå. Jeg skal skynde meg så mye jeg kan, for jeg vil fortelle om en afrikansk opplevelse. Og denne broren som sitter her fra Afrika kan forstå nå.
Jeg husket dette, at jeg sa, "Jeg skulle ikke ha noe mer med det å gjøre." Og en dag, på vei opp veien for å patruljere ved Henryville State Forestry, tok jeg bussen, og nå, hør alle sammen, nøye. Det merkelige var at hver gang jeg møtte en demonbesatt person, som en spåkone eller lignende, så snakket de til meg og la merke til noe. Og det skremte meg til døde.

text:
I remember going out one night, at a carnival, I was just about eighteen years old. My cousin, a couple of them, and I, were going down. There was a little old gypsy fortune tellers tent sitting there. And I was going down along the side there, and the little gypsy woman said, "Say, you, come here!" Well, all three of turned around. She said, "You with the striped sweater on."
I said, "Me?"
"Yes!"
I thought maybe she wanted me to get her a Coke. And I said, "Yes, madam."
She said, "Do you know that you were born under a sign?"
I said, "Look woman. Shut up!" And I got away from there right quick. Then oh, they begin to tease me, go on.
And one day, while I was warden, I was going up on the bus, and I standing there. And I always was subject to spirits. I felt a strange feeling. I looked around, big strong woman sitting there, well dressed. She looked up at me and she said, "Howdy do."
And I said, "Good evening." Looked up like that.
And she said, "You're lonesome, aren't you?"
And I said, "No, madam."
She said, "Well, you're not at your home."
I said, "As much home as I got."
She said, "You were born for the West."
I said, "Say, what are you talking about?"
She said, "Maybe I better explain myself." Said, "Are you an officer?"
I said, "Conservation!"
And she said, "Maybe, I better explain myself." She said, "I'm an astrologist."
And I thought, "Oh! Another one of them funny people." So then…
I said, "Yes, madam." And I just kept looking … I moved a little closer. There was a sailor standing right behind me, like that, the bus was loaded pretty well. I thought, "It was just a woman wanting to talk."
And she said, "I'd like to talk to you a few minutes." And I never acted like I heard her. I just kept looking on you know. And so she said, "Could I speak to you just a moment?"
text_no:
Jeg husker en gang jeg var på en karneval, jeg var omtrent atten år gammel. Min kusine, et par andre og jeg var på vei dit. Det var et lite telt der en sigøyner forutså fremtiden. Vi gikk forbi, og den lille sigøynerkvinnen sa: "Du der, kom hit!" Vi alle tre snudde oss rundt. Hun sa: "Du med den stripete genseren."
Jeg svarte: "Meg?"
"Ja!"
Jeg trodde kanskje hun ville at jeg skulle kjøpe en Cola til henne, så jeg sa: "Ja, frue."
Hun sa: "Vet du at du ble født under et tegn?"
Jeg sa: "Se her, kvinne. Ti still!" og skyndte meg bort. De andre begynte å erte meg om dette etterpå.
En dag, mens jeg var på bussen, følte jeg en merkelig stemning. Jeg så meg rundt og så en stor, sterk kvinne som satt der, velkledd. Hun så opp på meg og sa: "God dag."
Jeg svarte: "God kveld," og så opp.
Hun sa: "Du er ensom, ikke sant?"
Jeg svarte: "Nei, frue."
Hun sa: "Du er ikke hjemme."
Jeg svarte: "Så hjemme som jeg kan være."
Hun sa: "Du var ment for vesten."
Jeg sa: "Hva snakker du om?"
Hun sa: "Kanskje jeg burde forklare meg." Så spurte hun: "Er du offiser?"
Jeg sa: "Naturvakt."
Hun sa: "Jeg er en astrolog."
Jeg tenkte: "Å nei, enda en av disse rare menneskene."
Jeg sa: "Ja, frue," mens jeg fortsatt kikket rundt. Jeg flyttet meg litt nærmere. Det stod en sjømann rett bak meg, og bussen var ganske full. Jeg trodde bare hun ville snakke.
Hun sa: "Jeg vil gjerne snakke med deg noen minutter." Jeg later som jeg ikke hørte henne og fortsatte å se rundt.
Hun sa: "Kunne jeg få snakke med deg et øyeblikk?"

text:
And I thought, "That's not gentlemen-like, but I--I don't want to talk to her, see." And I waited there. Then she said, "Say, you the conservation officer," said, "could I speak to you, just a minute?"
I said, "What did you want?"
She said, "Could I speak to you just a moment?"
I said, "What do you want?"
And she said, I thought, "That was nasty of you to ask of a man." So, just so indifferent, you know. And she said, "Are you a Christian?"
And I said, "No. What's that to you?" See?
And she said, she said, "Well, I just wondered." And she said, "Did you know you were born under a sign?"
Oh, my! I said, "Look, lady, I don't want to know nothing about that. See?" I said, "Not giving you any short answer, I've got a mother at home, see." And I said, "I don't want to know nothing about that."
She said, "Oh, don't be so hard."
And I said, "Well, I don't want to know nothing about that stuff. See?" I said, "I know nothing about it, I know nothing about any religious things, and I--I don't want to know anything about it." And I said, "I don't want to hurt your feelings." I just keep looking on ahead.
She said, "My!" And she said, "You shouldn't act like that."
And I said, "Well, I--I don't mean not to be a gentlemen; but I--I…"
She said, "Look! This is nothing to do with religion." She said, "I'm on my road to Chicago." We was on a Greyhound bus. She said, "I'm going to see my son, which is a Baptist minister." She said, "I work in the White House." And she said, "Did you know the first thing, did you know this United States and all the astronomy, and how they do?"
I said, "I know nothing about it, see."
She said, "Well, I work in the White House." And she said, "Right as you go in the steps of the White House, there's the [unclear words]. If the moon doesn't control the tides."
I said, "I got sense enough to know that, see."
And she said, "Well, that's right." Said, "And your birth has something… Said, "Perhaps, I will tell you just exactly when you was born. Would you believe me?"
And I said, "You can't do it in the first place." See?
She said, "Oh, yes, I can."
I said, "Let's hear you."
Said, "You was born on April the 6th at five o'clock in the morning, in 1909."
I said, "That's right." I said, "Tell this sailor when he was born."
She said, "I couldn't do it."
"Well," I said, "why can't you tell him, if you just … if you could tell me?"
She said, "Because you were borned under a sign, I don't know when that was to appear." She said, "Has ministers never talked to you?"
text_no:
Jeg tenkte: "Det er ikke særlig fint gjort, men jeg vil ikke snakke med henne." Så jeg ventet der. Da sa hun: "Unnskyld, er du naturvernsoffiser? Kan jeg snakke med deg et øyeblikk?"
Jeg svarte: "Hva vil du?"
Hun sa: "Kan jeg snakke med deg et øyeblikk?"
Jeg gjentok: "Hva vil du?"
Hun svarte, og jeg tenkte: "Det var frekt å spørre en mann om." Så likegyldig, vet du. Hun sa: "Er du en kristen?"
Jeg svarte: "Nei, hva angår det deg?"
Hun sa: "Vel, jeg bare lurte." Så sa hun: "Visste du at du ble født under et tegn?"
Å kjære! Jeg sa: "Se her, frue, jeg vil ikke vite noe om det der." Så la jeg til: "Ikke for å være uhøflig, men jeg har en mor hjemme." Jeg sa igjen: "Jeg vil ikke vite noe om det."
Hun sa: "Å, ikke vær så hard."
Jeg sa: "Vel, jeg vil ikke vite noe om sånt. Jeg vet ingenting om det, og jeg vil ikke vite noe om det." Jeg sa: "Jeg vil ikke såre dine følelser," og fortsatte å se framover.
Hun sa: "Min Gud!" Så tilføyde hun: "Du burde ikke oppføre deg slik."
Jeg svarte: "Vel, jeg mener ikke å være uforskammet, men jeg..."
Hun sa: "Se her! Dette har ingenting med religion å gjøre." Hun sa: "Jeg er på vei til Chicago." Vi var på en Greyhound-buss. Hun sa: "Jeg skal besøke min sønn, som er en Baptisk prest. Jeg jobber i Det Hvite Hus." Hun fortsatte: "Visste du at USA, astronomi og alt det der?"
Jeg svarte: "Jeg vet ingenting om det."
Hun sa: "Vel, jeg jobber i Det Hvite Hus. Når du går opp trappene til Det Hvite Hus, er det [uklare ord]. Hvis månen ikke kontrollerer tidevannet."
Jeg sa: "Jeg har vett nok til å vite det."
Hun svarte: "Det stemmer. Og din fødsel har noe... Kanskje jeg kan fortelle deg akkurat når du ble født. Vil du tro meg?"
Jeg sa: "Du kan ikke gjøre det i det hele tatt."
Hun sa: "Å, jo, det kan jeg."
Jeg sa: "La oss høre."
Hun sa: "Du ble født 6. april klokken fem om morgenen i 1909."
Jeg svarte: "Det stemmer." Så sa jeg: "Fortell denne sjømenn når han ble født."
Hun sa: "Det kan jeg ikke."
"Vel," sa jeg, "hvorfor kan du ikke fortelle det, hvis du kunne fortelle meg?"
Hun sa: "Fordi du ble født under et tegn, jeg vet ikke når det skulle vises." Så spurte hun: "Har prester aldri snakket med deg?"

text:
I said, "I have nothing to do with preachers." I said, "I don't have nothing at all to do with them, at all." I'd always run from it, you know. Thinking of that, speaking to me, and I knowed that was the same thing, my mama, told me them things was of the Devil, and I stayed away from them. See?" And I said, "No, sir. I have nothing to do with it."
And she said, "Well nobody ever told you that?" She said, "Isn't it strange that preachers wouldn't know that?"
I said, "I don't fool around where they're at." And I said, "I--I thank you very much."
She said, "Well, look. You were born under a sign, I want to tell you this, as a gift." And she said, "If you'd only could recognize it."
I said, "Yes, madam." I said, "Maybe, I will be a Daniel Boone, I like to hunt and I was born in Kentucky." And so… She said, like that.
And said, "No," she called me. She said, "No, that ain't what I'm talking about."
I said, "Well, maybe, I'd be a businessman. I've got a grammar school education."
She said, "That's not what I'm talking about. I don't know what you'd be. But I know that according to astronomy that on that time, they was…
She tried to tell me about the [unclear words] of the cycles, and, in commemoration of the Son of God, when three stars came together. She said, "Three wise men, the magis were following three different stars, they didn't know when they were coming. Said, "When gathered together met each other at the Bethlehem gate, following three different stars, and they formed the one morning star, that hung over the Christ." And she said, "One was from the lineage of Ham, Shem, and Japheth, the three sons of Noah." And said, "Those three stars went together and made the one star that hung over Christ." And said, "Then when they separated, they've never went to their orbits, they've never been [unclear words]. Said, "Now all the star gazers in the country never seen such as that. And they never noticed it, it was just supernaturally given," and on talking like that, with all of her stuff.
Well, I couldn't understand what she was talking about. And she said, "In commemoration of that greatest gift that God ever give the earth, He sends back a something lesser in commemoration of that time."
"Well," I said, "lady, I am not a Christian. I have nothing to do with preachers. I have nothing to do with what you are, don't know nothing about the stars, all I know that I'm the game warden of Indiana, I'm doing the best that I can. Thank you. And went on, like that.
text_no:
Jeg sa: "Jeg har ingenting å gjøre med forkynnere." Jeg sa: "Jeg har absolutt ingenting med dem å gjøre." Jeg hadde alltid unngått det, vet du. Tanken på det, det som talte til meg, og jeg visste at det var det samme som min mamma fortalte: at slike ting var av djevelen, og jeg holdt meg unna dem. Forstår du?" Og jeg sa: "Nei, jeg har ingenting med det å gjøre."
Hun sa: "Vel, er det ingen som har fortalt deg det?" Hun sa: "Er det ikke merkelig at forkynnere ikke vet det?"
Jeg sa: "Jeg holder meg unna der de er." Og jeg sa: "Takk skal du ha."
Hun sa: "Se her. Du ble født under et tegn, som en gave." Hun sa: "Hvis du bare kunne gjenkjenne det."
Jeg sa: "Ja, madam." Jeg sa: "Kanskje jeg vil bli en Daniel Boone; jeg liker å jakte og jeg ble født i Kentucky." Og hun sa noe slikt.
Hun sa: "Nei," og hun ba meg komme nærmere. Hun sa: "Nei, det er ikke det jeg snakker om."
Jeg sa: "Vel, kanskje jeg blir forretningsmann. Jeg har en grunnskoleutdanning."
Hun sa: "Det er ikke det jeg snakker om. Jeg vet ikke hva du vil bli. Men jeg vet at ifølge astronomien på den tiden, var det..."
Hun prøvde å fortelle meg om syklusene og til minne om Guds Sønn, da tre stjerner kom sammen. Hun sa: "Tre vise menn, magiene, fulgte tre forskjellige stjerner. De visste ikke når de kom. De møttes ved Betlehem-porten, etter å ha fulgt tre forskjellige stjerner, og de dannet morgenstjernen som hang over Kristus." Hun sa: "En var fra Ham, Sem og Jafet, Noas tre sønner." Og hun sa: "Disse tre stjernene gikk sammen og dannet stjernen som hang over Kristus." Hun sa: "Da de skilte lag, har de aldri gått tilbake til sine baner." Hun sa: "Nå har alle stjernegazere i landet aldri sett noe slikt. Og de la aldri merke til det; det var bare overnaturlig gitt," og fortsatte å snakke om slike ting.
Vel, jeg kunne ikke forstå hva hun snakket om. Hun sa: "Til minne om den største gaven Gud noen gang ga jorden, sender Han tilbake noe mindre til minne om den tiden."
"Vel," sa jeg, "dame, jeg er ikke en kristen. Jeg har ingenting å gjøre med forkynnere. Jeg har ingenting å gjøre med det du er, vet ingenting om stjernene. Alt jeg vet er at jeg er viltforvalter i Indiana, og jeg gjør mitt beste. Takk." Og gikk videre.

text:
Well, that's just about the attitude. But strange that that… Then I got, after my conversions, and so forth, on down, later on down in life, after conversion got started, then It kept getting worse and worse, and It got so bad on me, I'd pray, and say, "O God, take this thing away. I don't want to see it no more, never. God, I'm a Christian now. I belong to You. Please, don't let that thing happen to me again. Don't show me nothing like that, Lord. Just let me go like I am now. Please, let me just study the Word like I'm taught to do here, and study the Word." But It would continually come.
And one day, I come home, and taken off my coat was going around the side of the house. There was a man sitting out in front waiting for me, my piano from the tabernacle in Jeffersonville, her brother. He said, "I'm going up to Madison, this afternoon, Billy. Would you like to go with me?"
I said, "Nope. Can't do. I got to patrol." And I said, "I can't go." I was patrolling on the high line then. And I said, "I can't go." And I started around the house, and I started around the house, seemed like whole top of them trees, right there, Brother Ryan, where you and I've knelt to pray, come down like that, and I almost fainted. I fell back on the steps, and I stopped there. And I thought, "Hum!"
My wife run out, she had some water. She said, "Are you fainting, honey?"
I said, "No." I said, "Just a moment."
After all… He was … He come running in Mr. Gibbs, then he went on back again, went on back to his place. I said, "Go ahead, I'm all right."
So she said, "What happened to you? Did you get sick?"
I said, "No, honey. It's that same thing again." I said, "Look, I've got seventeen dollars, laying in there. I'm going to have a show down with God. I'm tired of this. I've got a place where I'm going up here, at Green's Mill." Where many of you know where my hiding place, where the FBI couldn't find me. Back in a cave, where I go, I go there when things gets bad, and I settle it with God in there.
I went back up into a place there to a little old cabin where I used to stay, when I used to fish, and hunt, and trap back there. I said, "Meda, I don't know when I will return home. I can't tell you. I maybe home in a … two hours, I may be home in three days. I may not be home before two weeks, or two months. I don't know when I'm coming home. But I'm tired of this. I can't live a prisoner."
I said, "Everybody is telling me, I'm going around, 'I'm a Devil,' and so forth like that, by that, and me, trying to live a Christian life. And in my heart I love the Lord Jesus, and expect to go Home to Heaven. And I don't want to be plagued like this."
text_no:
Vel, det handler om holdningen. Etter mine omvendelser og senere i livet, ble det bare verre og verre. Det ble så ille at jeg ba: "Å Gud, ta bort dette. Jeg vil ikke se det mer. Gud, jeg er en kristen nå. Jeg tilhører Deg. Vær så snill, la dette aldri skje igjen. Vis meg ingenting slikt, Herre. La meg bare studere Ordet som jeg har lært å gjøre." Men det fortsatte å komme.
En dag kom jeg hjem og tok av meg jakken. Jeg gikk rundt huset og der satt en mann og ventet på meg, min svoger fra menigheten i Jeffersonville. Han sa, "Jeg skal til Madison i ettermiddag, Billy. Vil du bli med?"
Jeg svarte, "Nei, jeg kan ikke. Jeg må patruljere." Jeg patruljerte høyspentlinjen på den tiden. Jeg begynte å gå rundt huset, og det virket som toppen av trærne der, Bror Ryan, hvor du og jeg har knelt for å be, falt ned. Jeg nesten besvimte. Jeg sank ned på trappene og ble sittende der. Jeg tenkte, "Hm!"
Min kone kom ut med vann og spurte, "Besvimer du, kjære?"
Jeg svarte, "Nei. Bare et øyeblikk."
Etter noe tid gikk mannen, Mr. Gibbs, tilbake til sitt sted. Jeg sa, "Gå du, jeg har det bra."
Hun spurte, "Hva skjedde? Ble du syk?"
Jeg svarte, "Nei, kjære. Det er det samme igjen." Jeg sa, "Jeg har sytten dollar liggende der. Jeg skal ha en avklaring med Gud. Jeg er lei av dette. Jeg skal dra til Green's Mill." Et sted mange av dere kjenner som mitt gjemmested, hvor selv FBI ikke kunne finne meg. Tilbake i en hule hvor jeg går når ting blir ille, for å gjøre opp med Gud der inne.
Jeg dro tilbake til en liten gammel hytte hvor jeg pleide å bo når jeg fisket, jaktet og fanget der. Jeg sa, "Meda, jeg vet ikke når jeg kommer hjem igjen. Det kan være om to timer, tre dager, to uker, eller to måneder. Jeg vet ikke når. Men jeg er lei av dette. Jeg kan ikke leve som en fange."
Jeg fortsatte, "Alle sier at jeg er en djevel, og slikt, mens jeg prøver å leve et kristent liv. I hjertet elsker jeg Herren Jesus og forventer å komme til Himmelen. Jeg vil ikke plages slik."

text:
I went back up there. I went into a place. I read the Bible, I cried… No light in there so I had to close up the Word, and go back. And I started praying, I said, "God, please. I'm a Christian. I love You. You know I love You. You know in my heart, and You know me better than I know myself. I love You. And I'm told by the clergymen, and the so forth, that the Devil, a spirit moving around me, and Lord, I don't want to have that around me. You know I don't. You know I love You. And I believe You with all my heart. So why do You let my life be plagued like this? Why would You do it? Why don't You set me free of that so I can go preach and feel free of that?" And on like that. And I was begging to Him.
I was long about two or three o'clock in the morning, I was praying, and crying, and begging. And I stood there a little bit, and I was standing looked out a window, went back. I thought, "Well, it won't be long till daylight. When daylight comes," I said, "I'm going to leave the cabin, and gone up in them mountains and get back up in my cave. And I will stay there." As Buddy Roberson, said, "Till He finds a pile of bones when He returns. Or something had to happen."
I stayed and I prayed, I walked back, and for the first time in my life, it seemed like something now here's… Hear it now. Not be vision, but seemed like something telling me, "Could those ministers be wrong? Maybe This is right."
Well I begin to think. I thought, "Maybe they are wrong." Well, I thought, "Well, then, if they could be wrong, how does these devil possessed people, fortune tellers, and things, telling me all about this, and them holy man of God don't know nothing about it?"
Then, this come to my mind. When Jesus was born, it was star gazers, Magi's that saw the star, and followed it to Bethlehem. Is that right? No holy man saw it; Magi, star gazers, wise man. And them in the … them on the towers of the astronomers, that watches the stars all the time. They never seen the star. But the Bible said there was a star there. It was given for the Magis to see. Is that right?
It had already appeared while I was baptizing down here, it's right at the end of Indiana, the other end of Indiana there, where thousands were standing on the bank, when I was standing there baptizing. I started to say, "Father, as I baptize this boy with water for the remission of his sins, then Lord Jesus You give him the Holy Spirit." The seventeen person, I started to baptize, a voice screamed from Heaven on a bright afternoon, coming whirling down, out of skies, here come this light going, "Whoooossssh!" The papers packed, "Mystic light appears over local Baptist pastor while baptizing." Come right down and hung over where I was and stood there. People fainted and went on, then went right back into the Heavens, and everyone of them standing looking at it. There it was.
text_no:
Jeg gikk tilbake dit. Jeg gikk inn i et rom. Jeg leste Bibelen og gråt... Det var ikke noe lys der, så jeg måtte lukke Ordet og gå tilbake. Jeg begynte å be og sa: "Gud, vær så snill. Jeg er en kristen. Jeg elsker Deg. Du vet at jeg elsker Deg. Du kjenner hjertet mitt, og Du kjenner meg bedre enn jeg kjenner meg selv. Jeg elsker Deg. Prester og andre har sagt at det er Djevelen, en ånd som beveger seg rundt meg, og Herre, jeg vil ikke ha det rundt meg. Du vet at jeg ikke vil. Du vet at jeg elsker Deg. Jeg tror på Deg av hele mitt hjerte. Hvorfor lar Du livet mitt være plaget slik? Hvorfor gjør Du det? Hvorfor setter Du meg ikke fri fra det, slik at jeg kan forkynne fritt?" Jeg ba og tryglet Ham.
Rundt klokken to eller tre om morgenen ba jeg, gråt og tryglet. Jeg sto der litt, kikket ut gjennom et vindu og gikk tilbake. Jeg tenkte: "Det er ikke lenge til dagslys. Når dagslyset kommer," sa jeg, "skal jeg forlate hytta og gå opp i fjellene til hulen min. Der vil jeg bli." Som Buddy Roberson sa: "Til de finner en haug med bein når Han kommer tilbake, eller til noe skjer."
Jeg ble værende og ba; gikk tilbake, og for første gang i livet føltes det som noe... Hør nå. Ikke en visjon, men det virket som noe sa: "Kan disse prestene ta feil? Kanskje Dette er riktig."
Jeg begynte å tenke. Jeg tenkte: "Kanskje de tar feil." Men så tenkte jeg: "Hvis de kan ta feil, hvordan kan disse djevelbesatte menneskene, spåkjerringene og så videre, fortelle meg alt om dette, mens de hellige menn av Gud ikke vet noe om det?"
Da kom det til meg. Da Jesus ble født, var det stjernetydere, magi, som så stjernen og fulgte den til Betlehem. Er det riktig? Ingen hellige menn så den; det var stjernetydere, vise menn. Og de på astronomitårnene, som alltid ser på stjernene, de så aldri stjernen. Men Bibelen sa at det var en stjerne der. Den var gitt for at magiene skulle se den. Er det riktig?
Det hadde allerede dukket opp da jeg døpte her nede ved Indiana-elven. Tusener sto på bredden da jeg sto der og døpte. Jeg begynte å si: "Far, når jeg døper denne gutten med vann for tilgivelse av hans synder, så gi ham, Herre Jesus, Den Hellige Ånd.” Den syttende personen jeg skulle døpe, hørte vi en stemme fra Himmelen en lys ettermiddag. Den kom hvirvlende ned fra himmelen, og her kom dette lyset, "Whoooossssh!" Avisene skrev: "Mystisk lys vises over lokal baptistpastor under dåp." Lyset kom rett ned og svevde over der jeg sto. Folk besvimte og var sjokkerte, før det steg rett opp til Himmelen igjen, mens alle fortsatt så på. Der var det.

text:
My first revival, I looked around. I didn't know. There it was. The business man of the city, a group of them met me that afternoon and talked to me after I baptized. I had to baptized five hundred out of my first revival. They said, "What does that mean?"
I said, "I don't know." I said, "I am a believer. I don't know. It might have been to you, the unbeliever. I can't tell you." But while in that room, I begin to wonder.
Look. All those man, nobody could say that them priests wasn't holy man in the days of Jesus. Is that right? They were holy man, good man, righteous man, scholars of the Scripture. But while they were in there arguing what kind of buttons they must have on their coats, some Magis was coming to worship the Christ, that had already been born. Is that right?
Look. And when His public ministry come, now listen, when His public ministry come, they said, "He is a Devil, Beelzebub, the chief of the fortunetellers, the best medium there is. He knows their faults. And He knows all these things. He is a Devil." Is that right?
Who says, that's the Scripture? Say, "Amen." ["Congregations says, "Amen."]
But the strange thing was that [unclear words] turned around and said, "No, He's not. He's the Son of God." Hallelujah! God will make the Devil testify, His enemies, that He's the Son of God."
That Devil said, "I know who You are, You holy One of Israel. I know You are, Thou art the holy One of Israel."
And the preachers, them holy man, said, "He's a Devil. He's a Devil."
The Devil said, "He's the holy One of Israel."
I seen it. I seen it when Paul and Barnabas went up there to preach one time. The preacher said, "Them guys are impostors. They are devils. They alarm people. Don't listen to them. They turn the world upside-down. They're wrong."
And the little old fortune teller followed them down the street saying, "Boy, a Man of God, the [unclear words] of life." Preachers saying, they are devils, and the Devil saying, they are telling the way of life. Hallelujah!
text_no:
Mitt første vekkelsesmøte, jeg så meg rundt. Jeg visste ikke. Der var det. Byens forretningsmenn, en gruppe av dem, møtte meg den ettermiddagen og snakket med meg etter at jeg døpte. Jeg måtte døpe fem hundre i løpet av min første vekkelse. De spurte, "Hva betyr det?"
Jeg sa, "Jeg vet ikke." Jeg sa, "Jeg er en troende. Jeg vet ikke. Det kan ha vært for deg, den vantro. Jeg kan ikke fortelle deg." Men mens jeg var i det rommet, begynte jeg å undre meg.
Se. Alle disse mennene, ingen kunne si at prestene ikke var hellige menn på Jesu tid. Er det riktig? De var hellige menn, gode menn, rettferdige menn, skriftlærde. Men mens de der inne diskuterte hva slags knapper de skulle ha på kappene sine, kom noen vismenn for å tilbe Kristus, som allerede var født. Er det riktig?
Se. Og da Hans offentlige tjeneste begynte, lytt nå, da Hans offentlige tjeneste begynte, sa de: "Han er en djevel, Beelzebub, sjefen for spåmennene, den beste mediet som finnes. Han kjenner deres feil og vet alle disse tingene. Han er en djevel." Er det riktig?
Hvem sier det? Det er Skriften. Si, "Amen." ["Menigheten sier, "Amen."]
Men det merkelige var at Djevelen snudde seg rundt og sa: "Nei, Han er ikke det. Han er Guds Sønn." Halleluja! Gud vil få Djevelen til å vitne, Hans fiender, at Han er Guds Sønn.
Den djevelen sa, "Jeg vet hvem Du er, Du Hellige En av Israel. Jeg vet at Du er, Du er den Hellige En av Israel."
Og predikantene, de hellige mennene, sa, "Han er en djevel. Han er en djevel."
Djevelen sa, "Han er den Hellige En av Israel."
Jeg så det. Jeg så det da Paulus og Barnabas gikk der for å forkynne en gang. Predikanten sa, "De fyrene er bedragere. De er djevler. De skremmer folk. Ikke hør på dem. De snur verden på hodet. De tar feil."
Og den lille spåmannen fulgte etter dem nedover gaten og sa, "Dere er Guds menn, dere viser veien til livet." Predikantene sa, de er djevler, og Djevelen sa, de viser veien til livet. Halleluja!

text:
Don't think I'm excited. But, I've got to a place that you can't listen to nobody. You got to listen to God. Let every man's word be a lie. I know more about that now than I ever knowed in my life. It don't make any difference what people say, it's what God says, if it compares with the Word, then it's so. If the goods is there to produce it, because God testifies of His gift.
You listen to the preacher, some time, you get yourself in trouble, some of them anyhow.
Notice. There they was all muddled up in their doctrine. And devils, I hear fortunetellers was the only ones, them, was not the only ones, but ones that was recognizing Him to be what He was, and recognizing the apostles to be what they were, man of God, fortunetellers and devils.
So Paul turned around and rebuked that spirit of divination from the woman, she couldn't tell no more fortunes. He didn't need the Devil to testify who he was. He knowed who he was. He was God's saint. He didn't need the Devil's help. But the Devil was telling the truth about him anyhow.
Jesus told that Devil, said, "Hold your peace, but come out of him." Yes, sir. He didn't need the Devils help, but the Devil was screaming. So many people talking about…
Now one of these days before I leave now, I'm going to preach to you demonology, and let you know what demons are. You don't realize how they come right face-to-face with God, and plead to come down and do something about it. Now that's Scriptural, besides knowing otherwise. How right here in the meeting at night, when one come to the platform with an ounce of faith, you hear another one out there screaming for help. The one standing here on the platform, maybe, is dying with a cancer, another one sitting up there with a cancer. And this cancer knowing that he's facing down here, if that woman would only believe, what I'm telling, and believe Jesus Christ, she will be healed as sure as the world. Then to throw that woman's faith off, that other demon screams down to her. And I catch between them, and say, "There you are. Both of you believe."
text_no:
Ikke tro at jeg er oppglødd. Men jeg har kommet til et punkt der du ikke kan lytte til noen andre enn Gud. La hvert menneskes ord være en løgn. Jeg vet mer om dette nå enn jeg noen gang har visst i mitt liv. Det spiller ingen rolle hva folk sier, det er hva Gud sier som betyr noe. Hvis det stemmer med Ordet, da er det slik. Hvis bevisene er der for å bekrefte det, fordi Gud vitner om Sin gave.
Når du lytter til forkynneren, kan du noen ganger havne i trøbbel, i alle fall med noen av dem.
Legg merke til dette. De hadde rotet til sin lære. Det var ikke bare spåmenn som gjenkjente Ham som den Han var, men også de som erkjente apostlene som Guds menn, spåmenn og djevler.
Paulus snudde seg rundt og refset spådomsånden fra kvinnen, og hun kunne ikke spå lenger. Han trengte ikke at djevelen vitnet om hvem han var. Han visste hvem han var. Han var Guds hellige. Han trengte ikke djevelens hjelp, men djevelen talte sannheten om ham uansett.
Jesus sa til djevelen: "Vær stille, og kom ut av ham." Ja, Herren trengte ikke djevelens hjelp, selv om djevelen skrek.
En dag før jeg drar, skal jeg preke for dere om demonologi, og la dere vite hva demoner er. Dere er ikke klar over hvordan de kommer ansikt til ansikt med Gud og bønnfaller om å gjøre noe. Det er i samsvar med Skriften, i tillegg til annen kunnskap. Hvordan en i møtet om natten, med litt tro, kan få en annen til å skrike etter hjelp. Den personen som står på plattformen kan være døende av kreft, mens en annen med kreft sitter i mengden. Og denne kreften vet at den står overfor en utfordring, hvis kvinnen bare ville tro på hva jeg sier og tro på Jesus Kristus, ville hun bli helbredet, like sikkert som verden eksisterer. For å svekke kvinnens tro skriker en annen demon til henne. Jeg står mellom dem og sier: "Der er dere. Begge må tro."

text:
Oh, we're living in the day of Christ. The power and the resurrection and the Holy Ghost. People are… Time to quit playing church. This form and ritualistic ideas is all right. The hour will come when you'll see that I've told you the truth. That's right. Of course, it's hard now. I know it is. But I speak in the name of the Lord. And I know what I speak of. God testifies the same, that it's the truth. All right.
Then in there, I thought, "Oh, then, maybe I have been wrong. Maybe, I have been wrong. I should have embraced, I should have said something." But, I knelt on my knees and I said, "God, if I have been wrong, and I turned down something that I didn't understand, trying to listen to what preachers was telling me. Maybe, if I've been wrong, down in here, I don't understand it according to the Bible. But if I've been wrong, You forgive me."
And I raised up and I was crying. I sit down, looked around. I seen a light flash in the room, little that young fellow that flashed that bulb. And I thought, "Somebody must be coming." And here on the floor was a big light, coming around. Right above it stood this halo, pillar of fire, moving along, and come walking through there came an Angel of God. Not imagination. He was there. I looked at Him, talked to Him.
Walked up, with His arms folded, a big Man, very fine looking. Looked like if, looked like if He would have spoke the world would have bursted.
Said, "I've been sent from the presence of Almighty God, to tell you that you were born for this purpose to pray for sick people. To go into parts of the world, and you'll be praying for monarchs and great man, and healings, and so forth will take place. And these things will be great. Congregations will gather and it'll cause a revival that's going to sweep the land."
I said, "Sir, I'm uneducated. I… They won't believe me." I said, "They won't…
He said, "As the prophet Moses was given two signs for vindication of his ministry, he will turn his hand to leprosy, and then healing it, and the stick to a snake, and so forth," He said, "so will you be given two signs. One of them, you'll put your hand on the person. Then it'll be given… Don't think nothing of your own, it'll be given to you. Then it will come to pass, after so long a time, if you'll be reverent, if you'll be reverent, then will be given to you, to know the very secrets of their hearts, and the things that's wrong with them."
text_no:
Vi lever i Kristi tid, med kraften fra oppstandelsen og Den Hellige Ånd. Det er på tide å slutte å late som om vi er en menighet. Denne formen for ritualistiske ideer er greit nok. Tiden vil komme da dere vil se at jeg har fortalt dere sannheten. Det stemmer. Selvfølgelig er det vanskelig nå. Jeg vet det. Men jeg taler i Herrens navn, og jeg vet hva jeg snakker om. Gud bevitner det samme, at det er sannheten. Greit.
Da tenkte jeg: "Åh, kanskje jeg har tatt feil. Kanskje jeg har tatt feil. Jeg burde ha omfavnet, jeg burde ha sagt noe." Men jeg knelte og sa: "Gud, hvis jeg har tatt feil, og har avvist noe jeg ikke forsto, i forsøket på å lytte til hva forkynnerne fortalte meg. Kanskje, hvis jeg har tatt feil, her inne forstår jeg det ikke i henhold til Bibelen. Men hvis jeg har tatt feil, tilgi meg."
Så reiste jeg meg opp, gråtende. Jeg satte meg ned og så meg rundt. Jeg så et lys blinke i rommet, den unge mannen som blinket lyspæren. Jeg tenkte: "Noen må være på vei." Og på gulvet var det et stort lys som beveget seg rundt. Rett ovenfor sto denne haloen, ildsøylen, og beveget seg. Der kom en Guds Engel gående. Ikke innbilning. Han var der. Jeg så på Ham, snakket med Ham.
Han kom mot meg, med armene foldet, en stor Mann, veldig fint utseende. Det virket som om, hvis Han hadde talt, ville verden ha eksplodert.
Han sa: "Jeg har blitt sendt fra den Allmektige Guds nærvær, for å fortelle deg at du ble født for dette formålet, å be for syke mennesker. Å reise til deler av verden hvor du vil be for monarker og store menn, og helbredelser, og så videre, vil skje. Og disse tingene vil være store. Forsamlinger vil samles og det vil utløse en vekkelse som vil feie over landet."
Jeg sa: "Herre, jeg er ukyndig. De vil ikke tro meg." Jeg sa: "De vil ikke..."
Han sa: "Som profeten Moses fikk to tegn til å bekrefte sin tjeneste, han skulle gjøre sin hånd spedalsk og deretter helbrede den, og stokken til en slange, og så videre," Han sa, "slik vil du få to tegn. Det ene, du vil legge hånden din på personen. Så vil det bli gitt deg... Ikke tenk noe av ditt eget, det vil bli gitt deg. Deretter vil det skje, etter en viss tid, hvis du er ærbødig, da vil du få evnen til å kjenne de innerste hemmelighetene i deres hjerter, og de tingene som er galt med dem."

text:
Many of you here know when that first one was the only thing was operating, and not the second. Is that right? But I prophesied it would there. And I said, "That's why I'm here." I said, "That's why I'm here." I said, "Now that's what I'm concerned about." I said, "I come here, Sir, and I've been told that that was the Devil, that I … that it was wrong. But just a few moments ago I seen it." Of course, that was His presence in the room that changed that, of course. Then it was…
I said, "I've been told that that was wrong." And He referred to me the Scripture of Jesus, knowing where Nathanael was. And the woman at the well, and Jesus claiming that a little world would see no more, and yet, He'd be with us, even in us to the end of the world. And that this life, and so forth was just a vindication and so forth."
Then I said, "I will go." And He blessed me and returned back. He never told me to heal the sick. He said, "Pray for the sick." But He told me, "Be [unclear words] secrets of their hearts, and the people might believe." See? Not nothing… Now that's a divine gift. Then from there it started. And you know the rest of it, how it goes.
Now to Africa, quickly. Now when I was standing in with Brother Bosworth on the platform, one night, in Houston, Texas where thousands was gathered. Raymond Ritchey come in here the other night, and looked around said, "Say, doesn't look much like your meeting, all these empty seats."
I said, "But Brother Ritchey, God sent me to [unclear words].
Said, "But then, I miss you with all--with all my heart."
I said, "All right, pray for me, Brother Ritchey." I said, "I'd talk you, but it's just a few…
He said, "I understand." He was standing in the hall. He went away.
text_no:
Mange av dere her vet at det første var det eneste som fungerte, og ikke det andre. Er det riktig? Men jeg profeterte at det ville være der. Og jeg sa, "Det er derfor jeg er her." Jeg sa, "Det er derfor jeg er her." Jeg sa, "Nå er det dette jeg er opptatt av." Jeg sa, "Jeg kom hit, Sir, og jeg har blitt fortalt at det var Djevelen, at det var galt. Men for bare noen få øyeblikk siden så jeg det." Selvfølgelig, det var Hans nærvær i rommet som forandret det. Så var det...
Jeg sa, "Jeg har blitt fortalt at det var galt." Og Han refererte meg til Skriftstedet om Jesus som visste hvor Natanael var, og kvinnen ved brønnen, og Jesus som sa at en liten verden ikke skulle se Ham mer, men at Han skulle være med oss, selv i oss, til verdens ende. Og at dette livet, og så videre, bare var en bekreftelse og så videre."
Da sa jeg, "Jeg vil gå." Og Han velsignet meg og gikk tilbake. Han ba meg aldri om å helbrede de syke. Han sa, "Be for de syke." Men Han sa til meg, "Avslør hemmelighetene i hjertene deres, slik at folket kan tro." Forstår dere? Ikke noe annet… Det er en guddommelig gave. Derfra startet det. Og dere kjenner resten av historien, hvordan det går.
Nå til Afrika, raskt. Da jeg sto på plattformen med Bror Bosworth, en natt i Houston, Texas, hvor tusenvis var samlet, kom Raymond Ritchie inn her for noen kvelder siden. Han så rundt seg og sa, "Si, dette ser ikke mye ut som ditt møte, med alle disse tomme setene."
Jeg sa, "Men Bror Ritchie, Gud sendte meg hit."
Han sa, "Men jeg savner deg med hele mitt hjerte."
Jeg sa, "Greit, be for meg, Bror Ritchie." Jeg sa, "Jeg ville gjerne snakke med deg, men det er bare noen få..."
Han sa, "Jeg forstår." Han sto i gangen. Så gikk han videre.

text:
And that night down there when a very clergy gentleman, sits up and put in the paper that I was an impostor, and ought to be run out of the city, imposing myself to be a Christian, or a man of God." He said I ought to run out of the city, and also like that, and he would challenge me to an open debate." Said, "I couldn't prove in the Scriptures what I was … [Blank spot on tape] out of the miracles of Christ, that even Lazarus, he died again. So he challenged that.
Brother Bosworth come, said, "Looky here, Brother Branham."
I said, "That doesn't bother me, Brother Bosworth. Sticks and stones may break my bones, but words never bother me." See?
He said, "Well, Brother Branham, Brother Bosworth seventy something years old, had a fire and fight in him." He said, "That's wrong and that ought to be taken up."
I said, "We don't argue with people. There's thousands down there to be prayed for, a big coliseum sitting full of people," I said, "my goodness, what's the use of arguing with one critic, out there? Let him alone while the rest of them is getting healing, let him go on."
Jesus said, "Let them go. The blind lead the blind, they'll all fall in the ditch."
I tell you, I ain't criticizing Baptist. But Baptist people, fundamentally, know what they're talking about as long as it comes to the Word. But they know nothing about the supernatural. And my brethren there's where you're loosing the blessings. You're right in words, I believe you, I agree with you in the things that you believe positionally in Christ, that the believer takes his place in Christ, by faith, and he becomes a child of God, by the grace of God. I believe that, and I will agree with you. But you done shook too much for this side."
Isaiah said, "There would be a highway, and a way." And is a conjunction. "And a way." It won't be called the highway of holiness. It'll be called "the way of holiness." Right in the middle of the road, on this side there's this…
text_no:
Den kvelden, da en anerkjent geistlig, satte seg opp og skrev i avisen at jeg var en bedrager og burde bli kastet ut av byen fordi jeg fremstilte meg som en kristen eller en Guds mann, sa han at jeg burde forlate byen og utfordret meg til en offentlig debatt. Han påsto at jeg ikke kunne bevise noe i Skriften, og refererte til Jesu mirakler, som at selv Lazarus døde igjen.
Bror Bosworth kom og sa: "Se her, Bror Branham."
Jeg svarte: "Det bryr meg ikke, Bror Bosworth. Pinner og steiner kan knekke mine bein, men ord kan aldri skade meg."
Han sa: "Bror Branham, Bror Bosworth er over sytti år gammel, men han har fortsatt ild og kamp i seg." Han fortsatte: "Dette er galt, og det må tas opp."
Jeg sa: "Vi krangler ikke med folk. Det er tusenvis der nede som skal bedes for, en stor kolosseum er full av folk," jeg fortsatte, "hvilken nytte er det å krangle med en kritiker der ute? La ham være, mens resten blir helbredet, la ham gå."
Jesus sa: "La dem være. Blinde leder blinde, og de vil alle falle i grøften."
Jeg kritiserer ikke baptister, men baptistfolk forstår fundamentalt hva de snakker om når det gjelder Ordet. Men de vet ingenting om det overnaturlige. Der mister dere velsignelsene, mine brødre. Dere har rett i ordene; jeg tror dere, og jeg er enig med dere i tingene dere bekjenner om vår plass i Kristus, at troende tar sin plass i Kristus ved tro, og han blir et Guds barn ved Guds nåde. Det tror jeg på, og jeg er enig med dere. Men dere har beveget dere for mye til denne siden."
Jesaja sa: "Det skal være en hovedvei, og en vei." "Og" er en konjunksjon. "Og en vei." Den vil ikke kalles "hellighetens hovedvei." Den vil bli kalt "hellighetens vei." Midt på veien, er det...

text:
Some people when they get born again they sit right in the middle of the road, eyes on Christ. Finally they get over here, so much knowledge and wisdom, till, oh, they know it all. You don't know nothing. And if you don't do that, you run off the other side to a bunch of wild fire and fanaticism. That's right. But right in the middle of the road, there's a true, sound, unadulterated Gospel and power of God. That's right. Right down through there, [unclear words] taking the kings and monarchs, where anybody can stand and search, and they can see that it's the of Almighty God, not a fanaticism, or not a formality. It's the power of God.
And there, in that, Brother Bosworth, said, "Let me discuss it with me."
Second day he come out, said, "I ought to be run out of the city, and he ought to be the guy to do it." Well, that just to me, if you'll excuse the expression, that showed me the man hadn't been to Calvary yet.
You never take a Christian… A Christian don't take that attitude. When you are born again you love your brother. That's right. So by their fruits, you know them. So I just went on.
Brother Bosworth, still he couldn't--he couldn't be satisfied. He said, "Let me handle it."
I said, "Not to fuss."
He said, "Brother Branham, I won't fuss; but, it's … We owe it to the society of this city, that they put this in the paper, and let them think that we're a bunch of nitwits and don't know what we're talking about. Let me challenge them.
I thought, "That's pretty spunky." Like old Caleb, of old, let me go take that city, see. I kind of admired the old brother. I looked back between us. I said, "All right, Brother Bosworth, if you possible me you won't fuss." Now he's sitting here listening at me.
And he said, "I won't fuss. I will just give him the Gospel."
I said, "All right."
So the next day, we got… The reporters wouldn't let him around the place where I was, the [unclear name] Hotel, they wouldn't let him to the room. So then they wanted to know what I was going to say about it.
text_no:
Når noen blir født på ny, holder de blikket festet på Kristus og sitter midt på veien. Etter hvert kan de bli oppblåst av kunnskap og visdom, og tro at de vet alt. Da vet de egentlig ingenting. Hvis de ikke gjør det, kan de rase over på den andre siden til en haug av villfarelse og fanatisme. Men midt på veien finner man det sanne, uforfalskede Evangeliet og Guds kraft. Der kan både konger og monarker stå og søke, og de ser at det er av Den Allmektige Gud - ikke fanatisme eller formalitet, men Guds kraft.
Bror Bosworth sa: "La meg diskutere det med meg."
Den andre dagen kom han ut og sa: "Jeg burde bli kastet ut av byen, og han burde være den som gjør det." Vel, for meg, om du unnskylder uttrykket, så viste det meg at denne mannen ikke hadde vært ved Golgata ennå.
En kristen tar aldri en slik holdning. Når du er født på ny, elsker du din bror. Det er slik man kjenner dem, gjennom deres frukter. Så jeg bare fortsatte.
Bror Bosworth kunne fortsatt ikke være tilfreds. Han sa: "La meg håndtere det."
Jeg svarte: "Ikke for å krangle."
Han sa: "Bror Branham, jeg vil ikke krangle; men vi skylder samfunnet i denne byen å sette dette i avisen, slik at de ikke tror at vi er en haug av dårlige tenkere uten kunnskap. La meg utfordre dem."
Jeg tenkte: "Det er ganske dristig." Som gamle Kaleb sa: "La meg ta den byen." Jeg beundret den gamle broren. Jeg så tilbake på oss og sa: "Greit, Bror Bosworth, hvis du lover meg at du ikke vil krangle." Nå sitter han her og lytter til meg.
Og han sa: "Jeg vil ikke krangle. Jeg vil bare gi dem Evangeliet."
Jeg sa: "Greit."
Neste dag ble Bror Bosworth hindret av reporterne fra å komme inn på hotellet hvor jeg bodde, [uklart navn]-hotellet. De ville vite hva jeg hadde å si om saken.

text:
But, then, the next day here come the man from The Houston Chronicle and all of said, "The ecclesiastical fur is going to fly." You know how the paper has to talk anything up. "Oh, I was challenging a debate with [unclear words] Doctor Best, and what he was going to do. And like that." So he come down to… He said, "I'm going … He went and hired some commercial photographers of the American Photographer Association, and said, "Go down and take six glosses of me, while I go down and skin that old man. I'm going to skin him, and [unclear words] and tack it up on the door, as a memorial to divine healing."
You imagine a Christian talking like that! I can't imagine a man born again talking like that. I will tell you that. That's what it's going to be [unclear words]. But there's that Orthodox Jew, Mr. Iris, a Roman Catholic, the two boys that worked in the studio. They come down and Mr. Iris, that said everything about me. A goiter left a woman's throat, standing there, by the boy. He said, "He hypnotized her." And oh, my, how he criticized me in the paper the day before.
So I come down that night, there brethren, there was thousands of them gathered, and that's when I realized what friends meant. They come from the east and the west, flew in by special planes, trains, and everything, coming to the rescue. It didn't make any difference then whether you was a church of God, Assembly, or who you was, the truth was at stake.
Brother, when the persecution comes, you'll see the great ransomed Church of God stick together like that, stone over every stone. You'll forget whether your a Methodist, or Baptist, or whoever you are, when the persecution rise, and communism shuts down, the Church of God will run together, stone by stone.
That Solomon cutting the temple, one stone was cut this way, and one cut that way, and all this way. But when they went to putting her together, she went placed in together, and made the church of the living God. You're little differences will be all forgotten then.
There when one thing that all the Full Gospel people believed in divine healing. They flew for as many as five and six hundred miles that day, by planes and trains to get in. What was it? The thing that they believed was at stake. And their fan here, that they believed, was standing there to face it. And there it was, they wanted to come see it through. Just take it with you. That's the way to do it. Hallelujah!
I'm a real Kentuckian, together we stand, divided we fall. The way Christians, if ever one time we ought to stick together, is now. That's now.
text_no:
Dagen etter kom en mann fra The Houston Chronicle og skrev: "Det vil bli bråk i den kirkelige leir." Du vet hvordan aviser må overdrive alt. "Jeg utfordret til en debatt med doktor Best, og hva han skulle gjøre." Så han kom ned, sa han, "Jeg skal ..." Han hyret noen kommersielle fotografer fra American Photographer Association og sa: "Ta seks bilder av meg mens jeg går ned og hudfletter den gamle mannen. Jeg skal hudflette ham og henge det opp på døra som et minne om guddommelig helbredelse."
Kan du forestille deg en kristen snakke slik? Jeg kan ikke forestille meg en gjenfødt mann snakke slik. Jeg skal fortelle deg det. Men der var denne ortodokse jøden, Mr. Iris, en romersk-katolikk, og to gutter som jobbet i studioet. De kom ned, og Mr. Iris, som hadde sagt alt mulig om meg. En struma forsvant fra halsen til en kvinne som stod der, av gutten. Han sa: "Han hypnotiserte henne." Og å, hvor mye han kritiserte meg i avisen dagen før.
Så jeg kom ned den kvelden. Der var det tusenvis av brødre samlet, og det var da jeg forsto hva venner betydde. De kom fra øst og vest, fløy inn med spesialfly, tog og alt mulig, for å komme til unnsetning. Det spilte ingen rolle da om du var fra Church of God, Assembly, eller hvem du var; sannheten sto på spill.
Bror, når forfølgelsen kommer, vil du se den store forløste Menigheten stå sammen slik, stein på stein. Du vil glemme om du er metodist, baptist, eller hvem du er; når forfølgelsen stiger og kommunismen slår til, vil Guds Menighet stå sammen, stein på stein.
Da Salomo bygget tempelet, ble en stein kuttet på denne måten, og en annen på en annen måte, men når de satte den sammen, passet alt sammen og dannet den levende Guds menighet. Dine små forskjeller vil bli glemt da.
Alle Full Gospel-folk trodde på guddommelig helbredelse. De fløy så langt som fem hundre mil den dagen, med fly og tog for å komme dit. Hva var det? Det de trodde på sto på spill, og deres forkynner, som de trodde på, stod der for å møte det. Og der var de, klare til å støtte ham. Ta det med deg. Det er slik det skal gjøres. Halleluja!
Jeg er en ekte kentucker: Sammen står vi, delt faller vi. Kristne, om det noen gang er tid til å stå sammen, er det nå. Det er nå.

text:
Then, when that night come, my wife said, "Now, honey, you shouldn't go down there, tonight, that anointing is on you. And don't go down there, because you'll just pollute the meeting for the night before."
I said, "Somehow, I feel I ought to go down." My brother was just taking me in and out, like my boy does now, and a couple of more man.
They said, "Brother Branham, I don't believe I'd go down." Said, "Cause they're bound to fuss, I know how those fellows are." Said, "They'll fuss and carry on."
Well they … I said, "I just feel like I ought to go. I will go way up in the balcony, and pull up coat up over my ears, and sit up there." I said, "I want to go down." So I went down.
They taken me in, went up there, my coat all pulled up and just sit down. And Brother Bosworth got up there, like an old patriarch, challenged. And receiving that challenge and Mr. Best about thirty just out of the cemetery. And so, I said, cemetery. That's right. And so then, seminary, if you wish to call that. Anyhow.
Brother Bosworth got up there, he said, "How about you?" He said, "How many different Scriptures written here, Mr. Best, of Christ's present attitude in the New Testament, towards the sick and afflicted. If you can take the Testament, or the Bible anywhere, and disprove anyone of them, I will walk off the platform like a gentlemen." He was afraid to take the paper up. Brother Bosworth was astonished at him.
He said, "Then I will ask you one question. You answer me yes or no, it'll settle the thing forever."
"All right."
He said, "Was the redemptive names of Jehovah applied to Jesus, yes, or no?" That settled it. If He's Jehovah-jireh, He's also Jehovah-rapha. That's all right. If He was God's provided sacrifice, if He isn't Jehovah-jireh, then He is not the Saviour, He isn't the provided sacrifice. That settled it all together.
text_no:
Da kvelden kom, sa min kone: "Kjære, du burde ikke gå ned dit i kveld, siden salvelsen er over deg. Ikke dra dit, du kan ødelegge kvelden for møtet dagen før."
Jeg svarte: "På en eller annen måte føler jeg at jeg må gå ned." Min bror tok meg inn og ut, som min sønn gjør nå, sammen med et par andre menn.
De sa: "Bror Branham, jeg tror ikke du burde gå ned." De fortsatte: "For de vil sikkert lage bråk, jeg vet hvordan disse folkene er. De kommer til å krangle og lage oppstyr."
Jeg sa: "Jeg føler at jeg må gå. Jeg skal sette meg høyt oppe på balkongen, trekke frakken over ørene og sitte der." Så jeg dro ned.
De tok meg inn og jeg satt der med frakken trukket over meg. Bror Bosworth reiste seg som en gammel patriark og tok imot utfordringen. Mr. Best, omtrent tretti, kom rett fra gravplassen, eller seminariet om du heller vil kalle det det.
Bror Bosworth sa: "Hvor mange forskjellige skriftsteder fra Det Nye Testamentet viser Kristi nåværende holdning til de syke og lidende? Hvis du kan ta frem Testamentet eller Bibelen og motbevise noen av dem, skal jeg gå av plattformen som en gentleman." Mr. Best torde ikke engang løfte papiret. Bror Bosworth var forbløffet over ham.
Han sa: "La meg stille deg ett spørsmål. Svar ja eller nei, så avgjør vi saken for alltid."
"Greit," svarte Best.
Bosworth spurte: "Er de forløsningsnavnene til Jehova anvendt på Jesus, ja eller nei?" Det avgjorde saken. Hvis Han er Jehova-jireh, er Han også Jehova-rapha. Hvis Han var Guds tilveiebragte offer, og ikke Jehova-jireh, da er Han ikke Frelseren, altså ikke det tilveiebragte offeret. Det avgjorde saken helt.

text:
Best jumped up and down, and screamed, and stumped, and carried on, and snorted, and got angry, smacked the preacher, and so forth like that. Preached a good Campbellite sermon there, and never said, talked about when… You [unclear words] divine healing? First Corinthians 15, when this mortal puts on immortality. Brother Bosworth said, "I believe that too, sir. But I'm talking about Divine healing now, in the atonement."
Then he got all puffed up, and said, "Bring that divine healer on!"
Brother Bosworth said, "Ridiculous!" Said, "Brother Branham don't claim to be a divine healer, he only claims to be able to help the people by praying for them." Said, "Preaching divine healing don't make him no more a divine healer, than preaching salvation makes you a divine saviour." So he went on.
And after while, they kept on. Brother Bosworth said, "I know Brother Branham is in the meeting." I was sitting twice the distance of this building, way back up. Said, "I know he's here. And if he wants to come and dismiss the audience, alone, let him come do it, but he is not necessary."
And the people begin to look around. I was sitting there like that listening to Brother Bosworth, and admiring him. And just as he said that, I felt the Holy Spirit move down. Oh, that same wind that come, "Whoooossssh!" I looked around to my wife, looked back to my brother.
He said, "Sit still, Bill."
I felt it again going, "Whoooossssh!" Something moving. You can call me a fanatic if you want to, go ahead and you'll settle it at the judgment bar of God. I felt Something moving. I raised up.
Harold said, "Bill, sit down."
I said, "Leave me alone, Harold, in the name of the Lord. He's near!"
text_no:
Best hoppet opp og ned, skrek, trampet, bar seg, snøftet og ble sint, og slo til forkynneren. Han prekte en god Campbellite-preken, men nevnte ikke noe om ... [uklare ord] guddommelig helbredelse? Første Korinterbrev 15, når dette dødelige blir kledd i udødelighet. Bror Bosworth sa: "Jeg tror det også, sir. Men jeg snakker nå om guddommelig helbredelse, som er en del av forsoningen."
Da ble han oppblåst og sa: "Bring den guddommelige helbrederen fram!"
Bror Bosworth svarte: "Latterlig!" Han sa: "Bror Branham hevder ikke å være en guddommelig helbreder; han hevder bare å kunne hjelpe folk ved å be for dem." Han la til: "Å forkynne guddommelig helbredelse gjør ham ikke mer til en guddommelig helbreder enn å forkynne frelse gjør deg til en guddommelig frelser." Og han fortsatte.
Etter en stund, da de fortsatte, sa Bror Bosworth: "Jeg vet at Bror Branham er på møtet." Jeg satt langt bak, omtrent dobbelt så langt unna som denne bygningen strekker seg. Bror Bosworth sa: "Jeg vet at han er her. Hvis han vil komme og avslutte møtet alene, la ham gjøre det, men det er ikke nødvendig."
Folk begynte å se seg rundt. Jeg satt der og lyttet til Bror Bosworth og beundret ham. Akkurat da han sa det, kjente jeg Den Hellige Ånd bevege seg ned. Åh, den samme vinden som kom, "Whoooossssh!" Jeg så rundt på min kone, og så tilbake til min bror.
Han sa: "Sitt stille, Bill."
Jeg kjente det igjen, "Whoooossssh!" Noe beveget seg. Du kan kalle meg en fanatiker hvis du vil, men du vil måtte gjøre opp for det ved Guds domstol. Jeg følte at Noe beveget seg. Jeg reiste meg.
Harold sa: "Bill, sitt ned."
Jeg sa: "La meg være i fred, Harold, i Herrens navn. Han er nær!"

text:
About that time people begin screaming, thousands of them down there, ushers formed a big line. I went walking to the platform. I said, "I do not claim to be a divine healer, and I'm sorry that Mr. Best, and none of you so bad of him. His mother loved him as well as my mother loves me. And that's all right. That's what we're American for and that's what they're dying on the battle front for, is so we can have our rights, and so forth. That's all right." But I said, "I disagree with him in the Scripture."
He said, "As a man," he said, "as a man, I admire you; but in Scripture, I disagree."
I said, "That's a mutual feeling." Went right on. So, then we're…
He said, "All right." He said, "Now, looky, he walked over there then after the little rally, it come about hitting the preacher.
So then, I said, "It's a shame, that people would try to debate such things, when right here." I said, "One thing Mr. Best says that I'd like to say, he said, He felt sorry for these people sitting around here with cancer and so forth. He felt sorry for them.'" I said, "I don't believe that's the truth. I don't believe he'd sincerely mean that, because right back there in that audience sets people that a few nights ago were sitting here sick and afflicted with cancer and [unclear words] just like these people are, and there they are well. And he's trying to deprive these people of the only hope they have of living. And then say he's sincere, and feels sorry for them. I don't believe it."
He said, "Oh, Baptist don't believe such things as that." He said, "Only you bunch of crackpots believes such stuff as that." Said, "A Baptist don't believe in divine healing."
Brother Bosworth said, "I beg your pardon. Just a moment." He said, "How many of you here in this church, in this building, tonight…
And there sit a whole slue of Baptist ministers sitting there, which Brother Ritchey just called them. Said, "Which one of you sit in here?" The Baptist church better watch then. There's thousands and thousands of members sitting there, see.
So he said, none of them standing, he sent himself, the Baptist Conference wasn't going to be responsible for standing, it was him himself. All right.
text_no:
På dette tidspunktet begynte folk å skrike, tusenvis av dem. Vakter dannet en stor kø. Jeg gikk opp på plattformen og sa: "Jeg hevder ikke å være en guddommelig helbreder, og jeg er lei meg for at Mr. Best, og dere, snakker så dårlig om ham. Hans mor elsket ham like mye som min mor elsker meg. Det er greit. Det er det vi er amerikanere for, og det er det de dør for på slagmarken, slik at vi kan ha våre rettigheter og så videre. Det er greit." Men jeg sa: "Jeg er uenig med ham i Skriften."
Han sa: "Som menneske," sa han, "som menneske beundrer jeg deg; men i Skriften er jeg uenig."
Jeg svarte, "Det er en gjensidig følelse." Jeg fortsatte rett fram. Så, da vi var...
Han sa: "Greit." Han sa, "Se her," og gikk bort der etter en liten samling hvor det kom til å handle om å slå predikanten.
Så sa jeg: "Det er en skam at folk prøver å debattere slike ting, når rett her." Jeg sa: "En ting Mr. Best sa som jeg vil bemerke, han sa han følte medlidenhet med disse menneskene som satt rundt her med kreft og så videre. Han følte medlidenhet med dem.'" Jeg sa: "Jeg tror ikke det er sant. Jeg tror ikke han mener det oppriktig, fordi rett der i publikum sitter mennesker som for noen netter siden satt her syke og plaget med kreft, akkurat som disse folkene er, og nå er de friske. Og han prøver å frata disse menneskene det eneste håpet de har om å leve. Og så sier han at han er oppriktig, og føler medlidenhet med dem. Jeg tror ikke på det."
Han sa: "Å, baptister tror ikke på slike ting." Han sa, "Bare dere gale tror på sånt." Han sa, "En baptist tror ikke på guddommelig helbredelse."
Bror Bosworth sa: "Jeg ber om unnskyldning. Et øyeblikk." Han sa, "Hvor mange av dere her i denne menigheten, i denne bygningen, i kveld..."
Og der satt en hel mengde av baptistiske predikanter, som Bror Ritchey nettopp hadde kalt dem. Han sa: "Hvem av dere sitter her?" Den baptistiske menigheten bør da være på vakt. Det var tusenvis av medlemmer som satt der, ser dere.
Så sa han, ingen av dem reiste seg, han sa selv, den baptistiske konferansen skulle ikke være ansvarlig for å stå, det var ham selv. Greit.

text:
Brother Bosworth said, "How many Baptist in here that goes to good Baptist church and has good fellowship, can say that they have been healed since Brother Branham has been here, by divine healing, stand up." And hundreds of them stood to their feet. Said, "What about that?"
You know what Brother Best said? He said, "Anybody could testify to anything, I wouldn't believe it anyhow." And walked away. Oh, my! Hallelujah! Watch!
Then I stood there and I said, "I do not claim to be a healer. When I was born they just told me that an Angel of God come near me. Here a few, two or three years ago, yonder, standing in a place, He commissioned me to go pray for the sick people. And so forth, which I'm to do, to pray."
And I said, "I've got a letter right here now, and a cable gram from King George of England who is suffering multiple sclerosis, who was that man healed here in Fort Wayne, which was a friend to his private secretary. And he sent two cable grams already for me to come pray for him, over there."
And I said, "King George of England, the highest king, the biggest, greatest king on earth, today." And I said, "God told me, that Angel that sent from Him, said, I'd be praying for kings and great man and so forth, like doctors and so forth,' that has been healed right now in the meeting."
And I said, "I claim that I do not know nothing about their healing, only as I see, God shows me by His Spirit, and if I tell the truth, God will testify of the truth. If I am a liar, God will have nothing to do with me."
I said, "If I am a liar, God will never back up a lie." God is all truth. Is that right? I said, "If it's a lie, then God will have nothing to do with it." But I said, "If I speak the truth, my Heavenly Father will speak of me." And about that time, here He come. "Whoooossssh!" Hallelujah! There He come dropping down in the building, right over where I was at. Camera men standing there snapped a picture. The one that taken the six pictures of Mr. Best.
I said, "He has spoken that's all that's necessary." I walked away.
Even Catholic people sitting there, watched and seen that vision, said, "What was That over that man?" And come give their hearts, said, "I want to serve Jesus Christ from this on."
Man looking in the audience, hushed. I walked on out. Mr. Kipperman went in, he said, "Well, what do you think that, Ted, or Iris?"
text_no:
Bror Bosworth sa: "Hvor mange baptister her som går til en god baptistmenighet og har godt fellesskap, kan si at de har blitt helbredet siden Bror Branham har vært her, ved guddommelig helbredelse? Stå opp." Hundrevis reiste seg. Han sa: "Hva med det?"
Vet du hva Bror Best sa? Han sa: "Enhver kunne vitne om hva som helst; jeg ville uansett ikke tro det." Og gikk bort. Å, min! Halleluja! Se!
Så sto jeg der og sa: "Jeg hevder ikke å være en helbreder. Da jeg ble født, ble det bare fortalt meg at en Guds Engel kom nær meg. For noen få, to eller tre år siden, stod jeg et sted hvor Han befalte meg å be for de syke menneskene. Og så videre, noe jeg skal gjøre, å be."
Jeg sa: "Jeg har et brev her nå, og et kabelgram fra kong George av England, som lider av multippel sklerose. Den mannen som ble helbredet her i Fort Wayne, var en venn av hans private sekretær. Han har sendt to kabelgram allerede for at jeg skal komme og be for ham der borte."
Jeg sa: "Kong George av England, den høyeste, største kongen på jorden i dag." Jeg fortsatte: "Gud fortalte meg, Engelen sendt fra Ham, sa at jeg ville be for konger og store menn som leger, og så videre, som er blitt helbredet akkurat nå i møtet."
Jeg sa: "Jeg hevder at jeg ikke vet noe om deres helbredelse, bare slik jeg ser, Gud viser meg ved Sin Ånd, og hvis jeg taler sannheten, vil Gud vitne om sannheten. Hvis jeg lyver, vil Gud ikke ha noe med meg å gjøre."
Jeg sa: "Hvis jeg lyver, vil Gud aldri støtte en løgn." Gud er sannhet. Er det ikke riktig? Jeg sa: "Hvis det er en løgn, vil Gud ikke ha noe med det å gjøre." Men jeg sa: "Hvis jeg taler sannheten, vil min himmelske Far vitne om meg." Og omtrent da, kom Han. "Whoooossssh!" Halleluja! Der kom Han ned i bygningen, rett over hvor jeg var. Fotografene som sto der tok bilde. Den samme som tok de seks bildene av Mr. Best.
Jeg sa: "Han har talt, det er alt som er nødvendig." Jeg gikk bort.
Selv katolske mennesker som satt der, så og så den visjonen, sa: "Hva var Det over den mannen?" Og de kom og ga sine hjerter, sa: "Jeg vil tjene Jesus Kristus fra nå av."
Mennesker i publikum ble målløse. Jeg gikk ut. Mr. Kipperman gikk inn, han sa: "Vel, hva synes du om det, Ted, eller Iris?"

text:
And Iris said, "I don't know." Said, "It has got me beat." They went on in and started developing the pictures. Kipperman, the Jew went upstairs to go to bed. And when he did, his father lived over the studio, Iris, was going to… The Catholic boy was going to try to develop the pictures. And when he got it, he put them all through, developed them, smoked a cigarette, pulled one of them out, it was blank. The one he was going to take of Mr. Bosworth, when he was skinning him. It was blank. The one blank, the next one blank, and all six of them was blank. Not a one of them showed, God met him, knowed who was boss. Then he pulled the next one out. Then to his surprise, there was the Angel of the Lord, standing in a flaming fire over where I was standing. He grabbed his heart, he looked back, he dropped the negative, he screamed for Ted. He run [unclear words] Look at this [unclear words]. It's the truth, Ted.
Said, "Maybe I've been wrong." There, that night even yet at eleven o'clock that negative went to Washington DC, to be copyrighted, brought back. And George J. Lacy, the best there is in United States, on research was brought from California, to Houston, Texas, for to take the negative under consideration. He kept it for days. He looked through the lights, he looked at the camera, it took everything, and 'fore he could sign, because he's a FBI agent. The best they got. Fore he could put his name to anything, he went down, and then at the day when it was going to call out, he said, when he come out in the room, he said, "[Unclear words] who name is William Branham?"
I said, "Mine, sir."
He said, "Stand up." I stood up. A red-headed man, rather hard-boiled at first. He said, "Rev. Branham," he said, "I had a good Christian mother, I was taught to believe." He said, "But you're going to pass out of this life, like all mortals." I said, "Yes, sir. I know that. But thank God, I'm ready."
He said, "But, as long as there's a Christian civilization," he said, "I've criticized your meeting. I've said myself 'It was psychology.'" But said, "Rev. Branham, the mechanical eye of that camera will not take psychology." Said, "The light struck the negative." He said, "I'm ready to sign my name to a document." Said, "It was there. And It's the only supernatural Being was ever photographed in all the world's history."
People begin weeping and crying. There it is. We got permission to put it in the book back there, and there we stand today with it.
text_no:
Iris sa: "Jeg vet ikke. Jeg er helt forvirret." De gikk inn og begynte å fremkalle bildene. Kipperman, jøden, gikk opp for å legge seg. Hans far bodde over studioet. Den katolske gutten prøvde å fremkalle bildene. Idet han gjorde det, røykt han en sigarett og dro ut ett av bildene, men det var tomt. Bildet han skulle ta av Mr. Bosworth, mens han flådde ham, var tomt. Det ene etter det andre var tomt, og alle seks bildene viste ingenting. Gud møtte ham og viste hvem som var sjefen. Så dro han ut det neste. Til sin overraskelse viste det Engelen fra Herren, stående i en flammende ild der jeg stod. Han grep seg om hjertet, så bakover og slapp negativet. Han skrek etter Ted. Han løp og ropte: "Se på dette! Det er sant, Ted."
Han sa: "Kanskje jeg har tatt feil." Den kvelden, selv rundt klokken elleve, ble negativet sendt til Washington D.C. for å bli opphavsrettsbeskyttet og returnert. George J. Lacy, den beste forskeren i USA, ble hentet fra California til Houston, Texas, for å undersøke negativet. Han studerte det i flere dager, undersøkte det med lys og kamera. Før han kunne signere noe, som FBI-agent, måtte han være sikker. Når dagen kom og han skulle kunngjøre resultatet, spurte han: "Hvem er William Branham?"
Jeg svarte: "Det er meg, sir."
Han sa: "Reis deg." Jeg reiste meg. En rødhåret mann, temmelig hard i starten, sa: "Rev. Branham, jeg hadde en god kristen mor som lærte meg å tro. Men du, som alle dødelige, skal også forlate dette livet." Jeg svarte: "Ja, sir. Jeg vet det, men takk Gud, jeg er klar."
Han fortsatte: "Så lenge det finnes en kristen sivilisasjon, har jeg kritisert dine møter. Jeg har selv sagt 'Det var psykologi'. Men Rev. Branham, det mekaniske øyet til kameraet tar ikke psykologi. Lyset traff negativet." Han sa: "Jeg er klar til å signere en erklæring. Det var der, og det er den eneste overnaturlige Vesen som noensinne har blitt fotografert i hele verdens historie."
Folk begynte å gråte. Der er det. Vi fikk tillatelse til å sette det i boken der bak, og der står vi i dag med det.

text:
The day after that, Brother Bosworth come showed me a skeleton picture. Said, "Brother Branham, Florence Nightingale, is calling from Durban, South Africa, come pray for her." Said, "She's the great-great granddaughter, something to late, Florence Nightingale of all the…
I thought, "There is another one those renowns."
I said, "Brother Bosworth, and all these things going, I can't." And I'm fixing to close in a minute. I said, "I want you to get the last now, so that you'll know, and you'll hear me speak something just in a minute, I want you to mark it down." I said, "Brother Bosworth, I can't go, the way things are now. I can't do it." I said, "Let's pray." We knelt down on the floor, he and I, my little girl, and my wife, and we knelt in the floor, and prayed.
And I said, "God, if You'll heal this Miss. Nightingale, then that'll be sign for me to go to Africa, because, I've always wanted to go down there, to that, them people. And that This to them." And I said, "If You'll heal her." I forgot about it. And weeks passed, six or eight weeks. The woman had to be held up. You seen her picture in The Voice of Healing, we got it here just… There it is back there in the book, also, just a skeleton standing. Well, it was…
She couldn't eat she had a cancer on the [unclear words] of the stomach like that, and drew it into the stomach. And a big malignant growth, that crawled. Nothing could go down. They give her glucose, till her veins collapsed, and that was all. And there, her laying there in that condition, dying; praying for me to come. And I said, "Lord, if You heal her… Weeks and weeks later, I landed in London, England. I was going to go down to see if the King George being in. So then, I heard them page me, and Brother Baxter went down there, he seen it was Florence Nightingale had found out, some way, I don't know that I was coming to London, and she flew in just a little bit ahead of me.
And when she flew in there, I went to the… They wanted me to go out and see in it, and you couldn't even… She couldn't get out of the [unclear words] thought she wasn't going to die. I had a minister take her to the house, after I went out to Buckingham Palace, back to Westminster Abbey, and the next morning I went into London. I will never forget this. Listen close now.
You couldn't go home if you was dismissed anyhow. It's pouring down raining. So, you, listen just a moment. Maybe it'll cool off for tonight's service. Thank God for it.
text_no:
Dagen etter viste Bror Bosworth meg et bilde av et skjelett. Han sa: "Bror Branham, Florence Nightingale kaller fra Durban, Sør-Afrika. Hun ber om forbønn." Han fortsatte: "Hun er tipp-tippoldebarnet til Florence Nightingale."
Jeg tenkte: "Dette er enda en av de kjente navnene."
Jeg svarte: "Bror Bosworth, med alt som skjer nå, kan jeg ikke." Jeg skulle akkurat til å avslutte. Jeg sa: "Jeg vil at du virkelig skal få med deg dette, så merk deg hva jeg sier." Jeg fortsatte: "Bror Bosworth, jeg kan ikke dra nå. La oss be."
Vi knelte ned på gulvet, Bror Bosworth, min lille datter, min kone og jeg, og vi ba. Jeg sa: "Gud, hvis Du vil helbrede denne Miss Nightingale, vil det være et tegn for meg om å dra til Afrika. Jeg har alltid ønsket å dra dit til disse menneskene." Jeg sa videre: "Hvis Du vil helbrede henne..." Så glemte jeg det. Ukene gikk: seks, åtte uker. Hun måtte holdes oppe. Dere så sikkert bildet i 'The Voice of Healing'; det er også i boken, bare et skjelett som står.
Hun kunne ikke spise. Hun hadde en kreftsvulst i magen, en stor ondartet svulst som blokkerte alt. De ga henne glukose, men til slutt kollapset årene hennes. Hun lå der og ba om at jeg skulle komme.
